ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, ɔba fiɛ loto loɖe ara lonɛ ndã ne, maturi to maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Minyɔ ɖeɖei gu tarɛ̃dze gɔgbe! Ɔɖe ma lampofɔdze gu makpibaradze ɔlaa.’ Ɣɛɛ ne, Ɣaa iwola sɛ ite so i ɔturi nsɛgbai ame.” Simagɛ̃ Siwɛ̃ Maturi Iifɔ Yesu Maɖe.
translated: Long ago, there was a man named Awune Ɔbi, who had a child that was very sick, so he went to plead, saying, "My Lord, please come lay your hand on my child! So that he may be saved and live." And he said, "Sir, I am not worthy for you to enter under my roof." This is the Word of God from the Gospel of Jesus Christ.

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.

translated: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said unto his disciples, Ye know that after two days is the feast of the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.

text: So aaɣɔ mma i wũ kurabarara iita kuya iso.”

translated: Why are you sitting there without doing anything? I have been waiting for you since morning. Now the sun is already setting.

text: Mmɔ ɔto ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Ɔwi sɛɛ gɔmɔ ɔɔɣo. Ɣaa sigara ɔɖewi ɔɔbore ɔbo. Mifiniki si mifɔ Itɔ̃me Bielea nɛgbe miɖe.”

text: Ɣɛɛ maina kuiwɛ̃ atoa sɔ maturi gbodzoo ɔba maala mila masia wũ iso. Kama ne, marɛrɛ̃ inyɔ mawɛ̃ ɔba,
translated: I was going to the farm, but I met a woman carrying a child on her back. So, I turned back and came home.

ref: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.

translated: I am going to the farm because hunger has beaten me severely.

ref: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.

translated: If a stranger's blood is shed and we are called to come and see, we should not go and see.

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.

translated: If a person does not treat you with the kindness you deserve, you are not obligated to treat them with kindness either.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”

translated: If a person does not know how to pound fufu, they say, "The mortar belonging to Obi is crooked, that is why I cannot pound properly."

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, mala kulu maɣɛ sɔ, “Fɔ Ɣaa Ɔbi, be mpia i bo gu-ɔ ndɛ̃? Ɔba ɔɔba sɔ aba aakpadzɛ̃ bo kutsue aɖegu ɔwi gɔ masɛ katɔ̃?”
translated: Oluwe said, "I am going to ask Gaa Obi, why is it that he doesn't come? Is it that the child he has given birth to has grown feathers and wings that he doesn't come again?"

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.

translated: The elephant and the tortoise went to the farm to harvest cocoyam leaves.

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Petro ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ɖi aɖera ɔturi iɣɛra igbã nɛmɔ karɔ̃ te bo.”

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Maturi gbodzoo ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ. Gɔ mato maba ne, masɛgu mma lofɛfɛ̃ mafu, manɔbiɛdze, mabafã, mamumu gu manyɛse agbãagbã. Makɔ ma mabɔ Yesu katɔ̃.
translated: Thank you God for this day. I woke up well, I have good health, I have food, I have water, I have air to breathe. I am going to praise Jesus today.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,

translated: The old man who was clearing the bush has died and the one who was coming to inherit has also died.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.

translated: The children are playing in the yard with a ball that is very big and colorful. The ball belongs to the man who is coming from the farm.

text: Mmɔ ɔto ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Ɔwi sɛɛ gɔmɔ ɔɔɣo. Ɣaa sigara ɔɖewi ɔɔbore ɔbo. Mifiniki si mifɔ Itɔ̃me Bielea nɛgbe miɖe.”
